label: Wide-shell 1BR / 1 bath, LD style -- sibling of 1s_1br_wd_split_wing_bath_gr: same 3-column layout (west wet column, middle public column, east bedroom column), but the middle column's great_room splits into living (front, hosts entry) + dining (rear) stacked, mirroring the west column's own bath (front) + kitchen (rear) stack. Dining ends up side-by-side with kitchen (both rear row), living side-by-side with bath (both front row).

adjacencies.note: bath (front-west) and kitchen (rear-west) stack in the west column on one plumbing wall -- shared wall only, no door. Unchanged from the gr sibling.
bath's only door -- opens east into living near the entry, same role great played in the gr sibling. No bath-into-kitchen sanitary trade-off, same as the gr sibling.
living and dining stack front-to-rear in the middle column -- open mouth, no wall (ld style). Mirrors the west column's own bath/kitchen stack.
dining and kitchen sit side-by-side (both in the rear row, middle vs west column) -- open pass-through, no wall.

bedroom's only door -- opens west into living. door_placement: high_corner (2026-07-20, matching the gr sibling's fix) hinges it at the REAR end of the living/bedroom seam (toward dining), away from the front entry door -- previously defaulted to the front (low) end, clustering both doors' swings right next to the entry.
bedroom is full-depth, so its west wall also borders dining (rear) -- shared wall only, no door.

notes: Sibling of 1s_1br_wd_split_wing_bath_gr, created 2026-07-20 to answer
whether an LDK split works for med/max wide lots if dining stacks front-
to-rear behind living (as opposed to the side_split_bath_ld sibling's
side-by-side living/dining, which needed ldk_horizontal to bypass a
hardcoded solver conflict). Here living/dining occupy the same front/rear
row split the west wet column already uses, so kitchen ends up beside
dining (both rear row) and bath beside living (both front row) --
ldk_horizontal is kept regardless, since the gr sibling already needs it
for the kitchen-on-the-west symmetry-break override, which incidentally
also skips the living/dining/kitchen default-stacking rule entirely.
Layout intent (front at bottom):
Rear:  [KITCHEN | DINING        | BEDROOM (cont.)]
Front: [BATH    | LIVING        | BEDROOM        ]
[porch/entry]
FEASIBILITY (2026-07-20): infeasible at min (10x8) -- min stays the gr
sibling's job by design, this topology is med/max only, same split as its
side_split_bath_ld cousin. Feasible at both 11x8 (med) and 12x9 (max).
living needed mechanical_vent: true -- at 11x8 it came back with 0.00 m2
window area (living's only exterior wall is the front, entry door
apparently consumed the whole segment); same accepted opt-out pattern
kitchen already uses everywhere in this catalog, and the same fix
side_split_bath_ld needed (there it hit at max instead of med -- which
specific size trips the window check isn't consistent, so the opt-out
is applied unconditionally rather than size-gated).
No lot_adjustment_profiles yet -- untuned, both sizes solve clean without.
